repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

Fix https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39549

## Solution

- Adding more error checks on tests, small refactors on making requests
- Fixing `vim.net.HttpMethod` typo

comment_data.body:
nit: this is a bit strange, `exec_lua` can take a function which takes arguments, no need to do string contatenation.

just a note in case this becomes more complex later.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -37,22 +37,26 @@ local function assert_invalid_method(method)
 end
 
 local function request(method, opts)
-  return exec_lua([[
+  opts = opts or '{}'
+  local ok, result = t.pcall(exec_lua, [[
       local done = false
       local result
 
       vim.net.request("]] .. method .. [[", "https://httpbingo.org/anything", ]] .. opts .. [[, function(err, res)

comment_data.body: this could be an actual table instead of a string, if exec_lua wasn't using string concat above.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -212,50 +203,56 @@ describe('vim.net.request', function()
   it('accepts custom headers', function()
     t.skip(skip_integ, 'NVIM_TEST_INTEG not set: skipping network integration test')
 
-    local headers = exec_lua([[
-      local done = false
-      local result
-
-      vim.net.request("https://httpbingo.org/headers", {
-        headers = {
-          Authorization = "Bearer test-token",
-          ['X-Custom-Header'] = "custom-value",
-          ['Empty'] = '',
-        },
-      }, function(err, res)
-        if err then
-          result = { error = err }
-        else
-          result = vim.json.decode(res.body).headers
-        end
-        done = true
-      end)
+    ---@type table
+    local result = request(
+      'GET',
+      '{headers = { Authorization = "Bearer test-token", ["X-Custom-Header"] = "custom-value", ["Empty"] = ""}}'

comment_data.body:
a failure should just say what the failure was, not another long description
```suggestion
        ('request failed: %s: %s'):format(method, result.error)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -212,50 +203,56 @@ describe('vim.net.request', function()
   it('accepts custom headers', function()
     t.skip(skip_integ, 'NVIM_TEST_INTEG not set: skipping network integration test')
 
-    local headers = exec_lua([[
-      local done = false
-      local result
-
-      vim.net.request("https://httpbingo.org/headers", {
-        headers = {
-          Authorization = "Bearer test-token",
-          ['X-Custom-Header'] = "custom-value",
-          ['Empty'] = '',

-      }, function(err, res)
-        if err then

-        else

-        end
-        done = true
-      end)

+      'GET',
+      '{headers = { Authorization = "Bearer test-token", ["X-Custom-Header"] = "custom-value", ["Empty"] = ""}}'

-      vim.wait(2000, function() return done end)
-      return result
-    ]])
+    t.eq(
+      nil,

+    t.eq('table', type(result.response.headers), 'Expected result.response.headers to be a table')
 
-    t.eq('table', type(headers), 'Expected headers to be a table')
     -- httpbingo.org/request returns each header as a list in the returned value

-    t.eq(headers['X-Custom-Header'][1], 'custom-value', 'Expected X-Custom-Header')
-    t.eq(headers['Empty'][1], '', 'Expected Empty header')
+    t.eq(
+      'Bearer test-token',
+      result.response.headers.Authorization[1],
+      'Expected Authorization header'
+    )
+    t.eq('custom-value', result.response.headers['X-Custom-Header'][1], 'Expected X-Custom-Header')
+    t.eq('', result.response.headers['Empty'][1], 'Expected Empty header')
   end)
 
   it('accepts multiple HTTP methods', function()
     t.skip(skip_integ, 'NVIM_TEST_INTEG not set: skipping network integration test')
 
-    t.eq(request('GET', '{}').method, 'GET')
-    t.eq(request('PUT', '{}').method, 'PUT')
-    t.eq(request('PATCH', '{}').method, 'PATCH')
-    t.eq(request('DELETE', '{}').method, 'DELETE')
-
-    ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: no-unknown
-    local post =
+    local function assert_accept_method(method, opts)
+      ---@type table
+      local result = request(method, opts)
+      t.eq(
+        nil,
+        result.error,
+        ('Should not raise error on %s request: %s'):format(method, result.error)

comment_data.body: looks like we lost the `retry` ? it's good to be explicit even if retry=3 is the default
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -63,24 +67,11 @@ describe('vim.net.request', function()
   it('fetches a URL into memory (async success)', function()
     t.skip(skip_integ, 'NVIM_TEST_INTEG not set: skipping network integration test')
 
-    local content = exec_lua([[
-      local done = false
-      local result
-
-      vim.net.request("https://httpbingo.org/anything", { retry = 3 }, function(err, body)

comment_data.body: should this use `t.retry` ? or how will the flakiness be resolved by these changes?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -37,22 +37,26 @@ local function assert_invalid_method(method)
 end
 
 local function request(method, opts)

comment_data.body: @justinmk i was thinking about adding a sanity check in the `before_each` section, to see if httpbingo.org is available but it seems too much. Since we already have a retry in the actual request, does it make sense to also have a retry on that layer as well?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -37,22 +37,26 @@ local function assert_invalid_method(method)
 end
 
 local function request(method, opts)

comment_data.body: @justinmk was just keeping the old convention used on those tests. Will rewrite it to use a function

comment_data.body: no, but we need to be sure that retries are actually happening. if it doesn't already the `net.request()` error message should mention `(retries: x)`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -37,22 +37,26 @@ local function assert_invalid_method(method)
 end
 
 local function request(method, opts)

comment_data.body:
why not use `t.retry()` ? that will give a better error message too. just throw an `error` and it will retry.

Note: `t.retry` won't be available from within `exec_lua`. So retry around it:

```
t.retry(..., function()
  exec_lua(
  )
end)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4,55 +4,42 @@ local skip_integ = os.getenv('NVIM_TEST_INTEG') ~= '1'
 
 local exec_lua = n.exec_lua
 
-local function assert_404_error(err)
-  assert(
-    err:lower():find('404') or err:find('22'),
-    'Expected HTTP 404 or exit code 22, got: ' .. tostring(err)
-  )
-end
-
-local function assert_wrong_headers(expected_err, header)
-  local err = t.pcall_err(exec_lua, [[
-    return vim.net.request('https://example.com', {
-      headers = ]] .. header .. [[,
-    }, function() end)
-  ]])
-  t.matches(expected_err, err)
-end
-
-local function assert_wrong_body(expected_err, body)
-  local err = t.pcall_err(exec_lua, [[
-    return vim.net.request('POST', 'https://example.com', {
-      body = ]] .. body .. [[,
-    }, function() end)
-  ]])
-  t.matches(expected_err, err)
-end
-
-local function assert_invalid_method(method)
-  local err = t.pcall_err(exec_lua, [[
-    return vim.net.request(']] .. method .. [[', 'https://example.com', {}, function() end)
-  ]])
-  t.matches('invalid HTTP method', err)
-end
-
-local function request(method, opts)
-  return exec_lua([[
-      local done = false
-      local result
-
-      vim.net.request("]] .. method .. [[", "https://httpbingo.org/anything", ]] .. opts .. [[, function(err, res)
-        if err then
-          result = { error = err }
+---@param method vim.net.HttpMethod
+---@param opts? vim.net.request.Opts
+---@return table
+--- Helper method to make a HTTP request with a 2s timeout
+local function request(method, url, opts)
+  opts = opts or {}
+  opts.retry = 3
+  local result = exec_lua(function()
+    local done = false
+    local result
+
+    vim.net.request(method, url, opts, function(err, res)
+      if err then
+        result = { error = err }
+      else
+        ---@type string|table
+        local resp
+
+        local ok, parsed = pcall(vim.json.decode, res.body)
+        if ok then
+          resp = parsed
         else
-          result = vim.json.decode(res.body)
+          resp = res.body
         end
-        done = true
-      end)
+        result = { error = nil, response = resp }
+      end
+      done = true
+    end)
+
+    vim.wait(2000, function()
+      return done
+    end)

comment_data.body:
@justinmk what is the easiest way to grab the returned value from that exec_lua execution wrapped by a t.retry? i am trying some approaches here but none of them are working:

Examples:


**1**

```lua
--- doing the assertion directly on the callback
t.retry(3, 2000, function()
  exec_lua(function()
    vim.net.request(method, url, {}, function(err, response)
      t.eq(nil, err)
    end)
  end)
end)
```
raises:

```
.../neovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/lua/net_spec.lua:256: retry() attempts: 3
test/client/rpc_stream.lua:72: Packer instance already working. Use another Packer or mpack.encode() if you need to encode from the ext handler
```

With the async behavior of the vim.net.request, how can we grab any value from `on_response` to assert on the tests using t.retry instead of vim.wait?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4,55 +4,42 @@ local skip_integ = os.getenv('NVIM_TEST_INTEG') ~= '1'
 
 local exec_lua = n.exec_lua
 
-local function assert_404_error(err)
-  assert(
-    err:lower():find('404') or err:find('22'),
-    'Expected HTTP 404 or exit code 22, got: ' .. tostring(err)
-  )
-end
-
-local function assert_wrong_headers(expected_err, header)
-  local err = t.pcall_err(exec_lua, [[
-    return vim.net.request('https://example.com', {
-      headers = ]] .. header .. [[,
-    }, function() end)
-  ]])
-  t.matches(expected_err, err)
-end
-
-local function assert_wrong_body(expected_err, body)
-  local err = t.pcall_err(exec_lua, [[
-    return vim.net.request('POST', 'https://example.com', {
-      body = ]] .. body .. [[,
-    }, function() end)
-  ]])
-  t.matches(expected_err, err)
-end
-
-local function assert_invalid_method(method)
-  local err = t.pcall_err(exec_lua, [[
-    return vim.net.request(']] .. method .. [[', 'https://example.com', {}, function() end)
-  ]])
-  t.matches('invalid HTTP method', err)
-end
-
-local function request(method, opts)
-  return exec_lua([[
-      local done = false
-      local result
-
-      vim.net.request("]] .. method .. [[", "https://httpbingo.org/anything", ]] .. opts .. [[, function(err, res)
-        if err then
-          result = { error = err }
+---@param method vim.net.HttpMethod
+---@param opts? vim.net.request.Opts
+---@return table
+--- Helper method to make a HTTP request with a 2s timeout
+local function request(method, url, opts)
+  opts = opts or {}
+  opts.retry = 3
+  local result = exec_lua(function()
+    local done = false
+    local result
+
+    vim.net.request(method, url, opts, function(err, res)
+      if err then
+        result = { error = err }
+      else
+        ---@type string|table
+        local resp
+
+        local ok, parsed = pcall(vim.json.decode, res.body)
+        if ok then
+          resp = parsed
         else
-          result = vim.json.decode(res.body)
+          resp = res.body
         end
-        done = true
-      end)
+        result = { error = nil, response = resp }
+      end
+      done = true
+    end)
+
+    vim.wait(2000, function()
+      return done
+    end)

comment_data.body:
sorry, you're right. bc `vim.net.request` is async a `vim.wait` would be needed inside `exec_lua` anyway.

p.s. the weird error message is because `exec_lua` runs in the remote nvim process whereas `t.eq` is in the test runner process.

comment_regular_data.body: Merging to unblock, we can continue iterating. Thanks!

title: fix(vim.net): fix integration tests
